research.title: CLAUDE.md & Claude Code Memory: The Complete Guide to Persistent AI Context
research.whyTrending: CLAUDE.md is the most impactful yet least documented feature of Claude Code. Every developer using Claude Code needs this but no comprehensive guide exists.

seo.suggestedTitle: CLAUDE.md & Memory: The Complete Guide to Persistent AI Context in Claude Code

gaps: No article covers the FULL system: CLAUDE.md + auto memory + settings.json + .claudeignore + skills as one unified context architecture
Most guides show what to put in CLAUDE.md but not how to organize across the 4-layer hierarchy
No real cost analysis of CLAUDE.md size vs token spend
Missing coverage of auto memory MEMORY.md and how it interacts with CLAUDE.md
No troubleshooting guide for when Claude ignores your instructions
Nobody shows a real production CLAUDE.md with before/after optimization
strengths: Official docs clearly explain the hierarchy
HumanLayer blog has practical writing tips

officialSources.title: How Claude remembers your project
officialSources.keyFinding: 4 layers: global (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md), project root, subdirectory, user-specific. Auto memory writes to ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/MEMORY.md

officialSources.title: Claude Code settings
officialSources.keyFinding: settings.json hierarchy: managed > enterprise > project > user. Controls permissions, hooks, env vars.

officialSources.title: Using CLAUDE.md files
officialSources.keyFinding: Official Anthropic blog post on CLAUDE.md best practices and structure.

expertInsights: Target under 200 lines per CLAUDE.md file — longer files consume context and reduce adherence
CLAUDE.md is read at session start; every word costs tokens on every invocation
Auto memory is enabled by default — Claude writes learned patterns to MEMORY.md autonomously
Directory-level CLAUDE.md files only load when Claude works in that directory (lazy loading)
Skills load on-demand, keeping base context small — move specialized instructions there
Prompt caching reduces cost for repeated content like system prompts and CLAUDE.md
/init generates a starter CLAUDE.md by analyzing the codebase

statistics.tokenSavingsWithSkills: 98% fewer tokens vs loading everything upfront

strategy.angle: Most CLAUDE.md guides show you what to write. I'll show you the full context architecture — how CLAUDE.md, auto memory, settings.json, .claudeignore, and skills work together as one system. Plus real before/after examples from my own projects.
strategy.title: CLAUDE.md & Memory: The Complete Guide to Persistent AI Context in Claude Code
strategy.excerpt: CLAUDE.md is read on every session start — every line costs tokens forever. Here's how to architect your entire Claude Code context system: the 4-layer CLAUDE.md hierarchy, auto memory, settings.json, .claudeignore, and the optimization that took my cache hit rate from 12% to 61%.
hooks: Contrarian: Most developers treat CLAUDE.md like a README. It's not documentation — it's a behavior contract. Every line that doesn't change Claude's behavior is a line you're paying for.
Statistic: I trimmed my CLAUDE.md from 800 tokens to under 500 and my prompt cache hit rate went from 12% to 61%. That's two-thirds of input tokens at one-tenth the price.

Practical: Your CLAUDE.md, auto memory, settings.json, .claudeignore, and skills aren't separate features — they're one context architecture. Most developers configure them in isolation and wonder why Claude keeps ignoring instructions.
outline: Hook: My CLAUDE.md was 400 lines. Claude ignored half of it.
The context architecture: 5 files that control everything Claude knows
CLAUDE.md deep dive: the 4-layer hierarchy (global → project → directory → user)
Writing effective CLAUDE.md: the behavior-first principle
Real CLAUDE.md example: before and after optimization
Auto memory & MEMORY.md: how Claude learns without you writing anything
settings.json: permissions, hooks, and environment variables
.claudeignore: controlling what Claude sees (and the token cost of not using it)
Skills as context: moving specialized instructions out of CLAUDE.md
The optimization playbook: from 12% to 61% cache hit rate
When Claude ignores your instructions: troubleshooting guide
